description: The outer part or surface of something; what faces outward. 'Lerua' names the outer face—what meets the world, what is seen from outside.
sound_symbolism: The word faces outward. 'Le' begins with light exposure, outer reached. 'Ru' rolls across the surface. Finally 'a' opens to the world, exterior presented.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo lerua' (the exterior). Opposite of 'muwi' (interior). What presents to the world.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Exterior design shaped buildings—facades that integrated ornament with structure, beauty with function.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Exterior and interior are not separate—what shows outward reflects what lives within.
